question:
Sheffield United FC vs. Queens Park Rangers FC: Both Teams to Score

Description: In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Sheffield United FC and Queens Park Rangers FC, scheduled for November 8 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Sheffield United FC and Queens Park Rangers FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.efl.com/

evidences.title: Sheffield United vs QPR Predictions & Tips
evidences.content: bet365 Square Logo # Sheffield United vs QPR Predictions Published on 5:47pm GMT 6 November 2025 #### Sheffield United to Win Reasoning Reason for tip Sheffield United look primed to end QPR’s poor run and take the points at Bramall Lane. The Blades have scored in seven of their last eight league games and are unbeaten in eight of the last nine meetings with QPR. The visitors have lost four of their last five and conceded 13 goals on the road, so another defeat looks likely. [...] QPR could not break their losing run last time out, going down 2-1 at home to Southampton on Wednesday. They were priced at 17/10 to win the game, but out-of-form Southampton’s run of three straight Championship defeats came to an end with this victory. Sheffield United have scored in five consecutive Championship games and have found the net in seven of their last eight league matches. They secured a 3-1 victory over Blackburn two weeks ago. They have lost just one of their last nine Championship meetings with QPR. Backing QPR’s opponents during their current run of four defeats in five Championship games would have resulted in a profit of 4.93 units. [...] A 2-0 Sheffield United win fits the pattern....

evidences.title: Does Coventry 7 (xG 1.27) QPR 1 (0.61) prove expected goals is broken? - The Athletic
evidences.content: Another contributor to single-game xG volatility is variability in goalkeeping performances. This is where expected goals on target (xGOT) can help. xGOT is a metric that estimates the quality of on-target shots, taking into account factors such as the angle from which the shot was taken and its placement within the goal frame to give an indication of how likely the subsequent effort was to find its way in. In most big overperformances, xGOT jumps well above pre-shot xG, pointing to stellar finishing. Advertisement [...] Duncan Alexander and a football fan discuss data, where it is used well, where it could be used better and why it's sometimes confusing As with any xG overperformance, our first port of call is to roll back the tape and look at the finishes themselves. Did Coventry just hit their efforts absurdly well? Looking at the shotmap below, it’s clear that speculative sharp-shooting played a big role in their result. Two goals came from outside the box, and the overall xG per shot stood at a paltry 0.07. [...] Advertisement Here, Coventry’s pre-shot xG was about 1.06, and xGOT lifted that only to 3.00, suggesting that QPR goalkeeper Joe Walsh should have prevented four g...
